question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: streamFileProviders()] Can an unprivileged external user, by causing a network partition that affects only some nodes' S3 paths (not all), cause the importer to achieve partial consensus on stream files (downloading from some nodes but not others), potentially causing the consensus ratio check to fail and halting all stream file processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a pendingConnectionAcquireTimeout on the NettyNioAsyncHttpClient to cause a scenario where, during a network partition, requests queue indefinitely waiting for a connection slot, and when the partition resolves, all queued requests execute simultaneously, causing a thundering-herd that overwhelms the S3 endpoint?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: awsCredentialsProvider()] Can an unprivileged external user, by causing a network partition that blocks only outbound HTTPS traffic to S3 but not to IMDS, cause the DefaultCredentialsProvider to successfully obtain credentials while all S3 requests fail, and since the importer has no mechanism to distinguish credential failures from network failures, it may enter a permanent error state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the S3AsyncClient does not have explicit retry configuration in overrideConfiguration(), relying on the AWS SDK default retry policy, so that during a network partition, the SDK retries each request multiple times before failing, multiplying the number of hung connections by the retry count and accelerating connection pool exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user, by inducing a network partition that causes the S3 endpoint to return HTTP 200 responses with an empty or truncated body, cause the S3AsyncClient to hang waiting for the complete response body, since there is no read timeout configured, effectively creating a permanent stall that is not detected as a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: streamFileProviders()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the providers list is built once at startup and never refreshed, so that a network partition that causes one provider's S3AsyncClient to enter a broken state permanently disables that provider for the lifetime of the application, even after the partition resolves?
